model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Gonzaga's elite defense, ranked 9th nationally with an AdjD of 93.9-94.0, is the decisive factor in this matchup against BYU's potent but defense-deficient squad, as the Bulldogs hold a roughly 5-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage (+28.16 vs +23.28) and a far superior 30-3 record compared to BYU's inconsistent 23-11. BYU's loss of Richie Saunders to a season-ending torn ACL removes a key two-way contributor and further weakens an already below-average defense (AdjD 102-104, 88th nationally), while Gonzaga has thrived without Braden Huff for months and enters with superior defensive discipline that should contain AJ Dybantsa and BYU's 10th-ranked offense. The combination of Gonzaga's defensive identity, deeper tournament pedigree under Mark Few, and BYU's depleted roster makes the Bulldogs a comfortable favorite in this former WCC rivalry matchup.

reasoningStep.title: BYU vs Gonzaga — West Round of 32
reasoningStep.summary: Gonzaga's elite defense and superior overall efficiency profile should overcome BYU's offensive firepower, especially with BYU missing Richie Saunders to a torn ACL.
evidence: Gonzaga ranks 11th nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with a +28.16 AdjEM and the 9th-ranked defense (AdjD 93.9)
BYU ranks 23rd/33rd with a +23.28 AdjEM and a below-average defense (AdjD 102-104, 88th nationally)
BYU lost Richie Saunders (Big 12 Most Improved Player) to a season-ending torn ACL, significantly weakening their roster
Gonzaga has gone 30-3 despite losing Braden Huff to a dislocated kneecap in January, demonstrating elite depth
The key matchup pits Gonzaga's 9th-ranked defense against BYU's 10th-ranked offense, but BYU's 88th-ranked defense offers little resistance to Gonzaga's attack

Gonzaga’s NCAA Tournament path only grows more treacherous the longer Braden Huff sits out | Dave Boling March 15, 2026 Updated Mon., March 16, 2026 at 3:44 p.m. X Email Reddit SMS By Dave Boling The Spokesman-Review My on-site scouting report on Gonzaga’s Braden Huff last week: Despite a stubborn knee injury suffered in January, Huff was still able to elevate and was dangerous at the rim. Visual evidence. Following the West Coast Conference Tournament title game, Huff was able to climb up a ladder and snip off a portion of the net at the rim with a pair of scissors. Beyond that? He was still unable to suit up and actually play. The GU star forward’s injured leg has already pushed past its estimated range of repair of between four and eight weeks. Entering the ninth week of recovery, Huff has been ruled out for the opening weekend of the tournament, starting Thursday at Portland’s Moda Center against No. 14-seed Kennesaw State . Their No. 3 seed, and opening weekend venue in Portland, is news that couldn’t be better for GU. But Huff’s absence affects the Zags’ future, and his doubt creates a bittersweet aftertaste during this time when celebration is the mode of the moment. Bitter? Obvious. Wondering what t

Gonzaga’s NCAA Tournament path only grows more treacherous the longer Braden Huff sits out | Dave Boling March 15, 2026 Updated Mon., March 16, 2026 at 3:44 p.m. X Email Reddit SMS By Dave Boling The Spokesman-Review My on-site scouting report on Gonzaga’s Braden Huff last week: Despite a stubborn knee injury suffered in January, Huff was still able to elevate and was dangerous at the rim. Visual evidence. Following the West Coast Conference Tournament title game, Huff was able to climb up a ladder and snip off a portion of the net at the rim with a pair of scissors. Beyond that? He was still unable to suit up and actually play. The GU star forward’s injured leg has already pushed past its estimated range of repair of between four and eight weeks. Entering the ninth week of recovery, Huff has been ruled out for the opening weekend of the tournament, starting Thursday at Portland’s Moda Center against No. 14-seed Kennesaw State . Their No. 3 seed, and opening weekend venue in Portland, is news that couldn’t be better for GU. arguments.query: "BYU" "Gonzaga" Round of 32 preview prediction 2026 NCAA tournament

modelTrace.content:
Now let me compile my analysis based on all the data gathered.

**Key findings:**

**Gonzaga (3-seed, 30-3):**
- KenPom #11, Torvik #11
- AdjEM: +28.16
- AdjO: 122.1 (KenPom, 30th) / 120.3 (Torvik, 37th)
- AdjD: 93.9 (KenPom) / 94.0 (Torvik, 9th) — elite defense
- Braden Huff (dislocated kneecap, out since Jan 15) — likely still out for Round of 32, with Sweet 16 return "on the table" per Mark Few. Gonzaga has thrived without him (30-3 record).

**BYU (6-seed, 23-11):**
- KenPom #23, Torvik #33
- AdjEM: +23.28
- AdjO: 125.5 (KenPom, 10th) / 124.8 (Torvik, 16th) — elite offense
- AdjD: 102.3 (KenPom) / 104.3 (Torvik, 88th) — below average defense
- Richie Saunders (torn ACL, season-ending) — major loss, Big 12 Most Improved Player
- AJ Dybantsa — generational freshman talent, healthy

**Matchup analysis:**
- Gonzaga's efficiency margin advantage: +28.16 vs +23.28 = ~5 points
- The key matchup: Gonzaga's elite defense (9th nationally, ~94.0) vs BYU's elite offense (10th nationally, ~125.5). This is the defining clash.
- BYU's weak defense (88th-102nd) vs Gonzaga's good-but-not-elite offense (30th-37th)
- BYU is missing Richie Saunders (torn ACL), a major contributor
- Gonzaga still missing Braden Huff but has been dominant without him all season
- BYU's 23-11 record with 11 losses reflects inconsistency; Gonzaga's 30-3 is far more dominant
- Former WCC rivals — deep familiarity, though BYU is now in the Big 12

Gonzaga's elite defense should be the deciding factor. BYU's offense is potent but their defense is a liability, and without Saunders they lose a key two-way player. Gonzaga's 30-3 record and ~5-point efficiency margin advantage, combined with the defensive matchup edge, make them the clear favorite.

BYU's loss of Richie Saunders to a season-ending torn ACL removes a key two-way contributor and further weakens an already below-average defense (AdjD 102-104, 88th nationally), while Gonzaga has thrived without Braden Huff for months and enters with superior defensive discipline that should contain AJ Dybantsa and BYU's 10th-ranked offense. The combination of Gonzaga's defensive identity, deeper tournament pedigree under Mark Few, and BYU's depleted roster makes the Bulldogs a comfortable favorite in this former WCC rivalry matchup."
